text.title: AI Workload Cost Attribution Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of ai-workload-cost-attribution enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Every LLM call carries a `tenant_id` (and optional `feature_id`) propagated end-to-end as an OTel attribute.
rule.rationale: Without tenant tag at call-time, attribution becomes post-hoc guesswork.

rule.statement: Cost-per-call is computed from (a) reported token counts × (b) a versioned pricing catalogue.
rule.rationale: Pricing changes between provider releases; pinning the catalogue version makes the attribution reproducible.

rule.statement: GPU-second cost is mapped to tenants via the on-GPU request-tag (vGPU + MIG or per-pod label).

rationale.source: DCGM exporter docs; NVIDIA MIG guide

rule.rationale: Without on-GPU tag, multi-tenant GPU pools become a `shared` bucket nobody owns.

rule.statement: Monthly rollup is reconciled to provider invoice with ≤0.5% variance OR a written reason.
rule.rationale: Unreconciled reports erode finance trust and are quietly ignored.

rule.statement: An `UNATTRIBUTED` bucket exists by design and is auto-flagged whenever it exceeds 2% of total — root-caused within the next cycle.
rule.rationale: Hiding unattributed cost as 'overhead' breaks unit economics; flagging it forces investigation.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
